title: AtomicBool single-registration guard with rollback on FFI failure
description: The REGISTERED AtomicBool uses compare_exchange to claim the global wolfCrypt CryptoCb slot before calling wc_CryptoCb_RegisterDevice, and restores it to false if the FFI call fails. wolfCrypt's CryptoCb table is a fixed-size C global with no thread safety; registering twice for the same DEV_ID would silently overwrite the first entry and leave the first Client's raw pointer registered without a corresponding guard to unregister it. The compare_exchange+rollback pattern correctly prevents double registration while not permanently consuming the slot if the underlying C call fails. A future contributor might suggest a Mutex instead, or removing the guard entirely and letting wolfCrypt handle it. wolfCrypt does not error on double registration, so the Rust-level gate is load-bearing.
